subsection.heading: In general
subsection.chapeau: Pursuant to the authorities and processes established under section 3719 of title 15, the Director of the National Institutes of Health shall support prize competitions for one or both of the following goals:

paragraph.content: Identifying and funding areas of biomedical science that could realize significant advancements through a prize competition.

paragraph.chapeau: Improving health outcomes, particularly with respect to human diseases and conditions—

subparagraph.content: for which public and private investment in research is disproportionately small relative to Federal Government expenditures on prevention and treatment activities with respect to such diseases and conditions, such that Federal expenditures on health programs would be reduced;
that are serious and represent a significant disease burden in the United States; or
for which there is potential for significant return on investment to the United States.

subsection.heading: Tracking; reporting
subsection.chapeau: The Director of the National Institutes of Health shall—

subparagraph.content: the effect of innovations funded through the prize competitions under this section in advancing biomedical science or improving health outcomes pursuant to subsection (a); and
the effect of the innovations on Federal expenditures; and

paragraph.content: include the information collected under paragraph (1) in the triennial report under section 283 of this title (as amended by section 2032).

note.heading: References in Text
note.p: Section 2032, referred to in subsec. (b)(2), means section 2032 of Pub. L. 114–255.

note.heading: Codification
note.p: Section was enacted as part of the 21st Century Cures Act, and not as part of the Public Health Service Act which comprises this chapter.
